# eyecite-ts

> TypeScript legal citation extraction library with zero runtime dependencies. Extract, resolve, annotate, and analyze legal citations from court opinions and legal documents.

Port of Python [eyecite](https://github.com/freelawproject/eyecite) by Free Law Project. This TypeScript implementation adds 23 citation types, parallel citation linking, party-name extraction, full-span tracking, structured date parsing, a document-understanding layer (quote attribution + a typed citation graph), and short-form resolution with confidence/abstention controls.

- Version: 0.29.2
- License: MIT
- Node.js: >=18.0.0
- Runtime dependencies: 0
- Module format: ESM + CJS, with `.d.mts` / `.d.cts` type declarations
- Repository: https://github.com/medelman17/eyecite-ts

<!--
  AUTOGENERATED by scripts/generate-llms.ts — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
  Regenerate via `pnpm llms:generate` (also runs in `prebuild`).
  Volatile facts (version, the CitationType union, the Edge union, per-entry
  export lists, option fields) are derived from src/ via the TypeScript
  compiler API. Curated prose lives in the generator. Written to both
  ./llms.txt and .claude/skills/eyecite-ts-skill/references/llms.txt.
-->

## Installation

```bash
npm install eyecite-ts
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { extractCitations } from "eyecite-ts"

const text = "See Smith v. Jones, 500 F.2d 123 (9th Cir. 2020). Id. at 130."
const citations = extractCitations(text)
// [{ type: "case", volume: 500, reporter: "F.2d", page: 123, court: "9th Cir.", year: 2020, ... },
//  { type: "id", pincite: 130, ... }]
```

## Architecture — the pipeline

Citations flow through four core stages, plus an optional fifth document-understanding layer:

1. **Clean** (`src/clean/`): strip HTML, decode entities, rejoin hyphenated line-wraps, strip PDF page-break markers, normalize whitespace/Unicode/dashes/typography, fix smart quotes, normalize reporter spacing. Builds a `TransformationMap` tracking position shifts between original and cleaned text.
2. **Tokenize** (`src/tokenize/`): apply regex patterns to find citation candidates. Intentionally broad; the next stage validates.
3. **Extract** (`src/extract/`): parse metadata from tokens (volume, reporter, page, court, year, case name, pincite, parentheticals, dates, disposition). Each citation type has its own extractor. Confidence scores assigned here.
4. **Resolve** (opt-in, `src/resolve/`): link short-form citations (Id./supra/short-form case) to full antecedents. Uses scope boundaries, BK-tree Levenshtein fuzzy party-name matching, and pincite inheritance.
5. **Analyze** (opt-in, `src/document/`): `analyzeDocument()` projects an already-extracted citation array into a `Document` view — prose spans, quote attributions, and a typed citation graph. Pure projection; no re-tokenization.

Position tracking uses dual coordinates: `cleanStart`/`cleanEnd` (internal parsing space) and `originalStart`/`originalEnd` (user-facing, in the raw input), connected by the `TransformationMap`. Use the `original*` fields for highlighting in the user's text.

## Package entry points

Four entry points. Import only what you need.

| Entry point | Import path | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| Core | `eyecite-ts` | Extraction, resolution, document analysis, footnote detection, types, type guards, the `cleanText` orchestrator, granular extractors |
| Annotate | `eyecite-ts/annotate` | HTML annotation (template + callback modes) |
| Utils | `eyecite-ts/utils` | Post-extraction utilities: reporter keys, Bluebook formatting, case grouping, surrounding-context extraction |
| Data | `eyecite-ts/data` | Reporter database (1200+ reporters) + jurisdiction/state-code registries, lazy-loaded |

There is **no `eyecite-ts/clean` entry point** and the individual cleaner functions (`stripHtmlTags`, `normalizeUnicode`, …) are **not exported** from any public entry — see "Cleaners" below.

### Exports per entry (auto-generated)

**`eyecite-ts`**

- **Functions** (23): `analyzeDocument`, `applyFalsePositiveFilters`, `cleanText`, `detectFootnotes`, `extractAnnotation`, `extractCase`, `extractCitations`, `extractCitationsAsync`, `extractConstitutional`, `extractFederalRegister`, `extractFederalRule`, `extractJournal`, `extractNeutral`, `extractPublicLaw`, `extractRestatement`, `extractStatute`, `extractStatutesAtLarge`, `extractTreatise`, `normalizeCourt`, `parsePincite`, `resolveCitations`, `spanFromGroupIndex`, `tokenize`
- **Classes** (1): `DocumentResolver`
- **Type guards** (5): `assertUnreachable`, `isCaseCitation`, `isCitationType`, `isFullCitation`, `isShortFormCitation`
- **Interfaces** (46): `AnalyzedFootnoteZone`, `AnnotationCitation`, `AnnotationComponentSpans`, `CaseComponentSpans`, `CitationBase`, `CitationGraph`, `CleanTextResult`, `ConstitutionalCitation`, `ConstitutionalComponentSpans`, `CourtInference`, `DocketCitation`, `Document`, `ExtractOptions`, `FederalRegisterCitation`, `FederalRegisterComponentSpans`, `FederalRuleCitation`, `FederalRuleComponentSpans`, `FootnoteZone`, `FullCaseCitation`, `IdCitation`, `JournalCitation`, `JournalComponentSpans`, `NeutralCitation`, `NeutralComponentSpans`, `Parenthetical`, `PinciteInfo`, `PublicLawCitation`, `PublicLawComponentSpans`, `QuoteAttribution`, `ResolutionOptions`, `ResolutionResult`, `RestatementCitation`, `RestatementComponentSpans`, `ShortFormCaseCitation`, `Span`, `StatuteCitation`, `StatuteComponentSpans`, `StatutesAtLargeCitation`, `StatutesAtLargeComponentSpans`, `SubsequentHistoryEntry`, `SupraCitation`, `Token`, `TransformationMap`, `TreatiseCitation`, `TreatiseComponentSpans`, `Warning`
- **Types** (16): `AttributionKind`, `Citation`, `CitationOfType`, `CitationSignal`, `CitationType`, `Edge`, `ExtractorMap`, `FootnoteMap`, `FullCitation`, `FullCitationType`, `HistorySignal`, `ParentheticalType`, `ResolvedCitation`, `ScopeStrategy`, `ShortFormCitation`, `ShortFormCitationType`

**`eyecite-ts/annotate`**

- **Functions** (1): `annotate`
- **Interfaces** (2): `AnnotationOptions`, `AnnotationResult`

**`eyecite-ts/utils`**

- **Functions** (7): `getSurroundingContext`, `groupByCase`, `toBluebook`, `toDurableLocator`, `toDurableLocators`, `toReporterKey`, `toReporterKeys`
- **Interfaces** (5): `CaseGroup`, `ContextOptions`, `DurableLocator`, `DurableLocatorOptions`, `SurroundingContext`

**`eyecite-ts/data`**

- **Functions** (5): `findAbbreviatedCode`, `findNamedCode`, `findReportersByAbbreviation`, `getReportersSync`, `loadReporters`
- **Values** (3): `abbreviatedCodes`, `namedCodes`, `stateStatuteEntries`
- **Interfaces** (5): `CodeEntry`, `ReporterEdition`, `ReporterEntry`, `ReportersDatabase`, `StateStatuteEntry`

## Citation types

A discriminated union on the `type` field — **23 types total: 20 full + 3 short-form**. Switch on `citation.type` (or use the type guards) for type-safe field access; TypeScript narrows automatically.

### Full citation types (20 discriminators)

`case`, `docket`, `statute`, `regulation`, `stateRule`, `journal`, `neutral`, `publicLaw`, `federalRegister`, `statutesAtLarge`, `sessionLaw`, `treaty`, `legislativeMaterial`, `localOrdinance`, `canon`, `constitutional`, `federalRule`, `restatement`, `treatise`, `annotation`

### Short-form citation types (3 discriminators)

`id`, `supra`, `shortFormCase` — appear only in text that references earlier citations.

### Notable fields per subtype

Field lists below are derived from `src/types/citation.ts`; the prose note per subtype is curated.

- `case` — `FullCaseCitation`: the workhorse — volume/reporter/page plus case name, parties, court, year, parentheticals, parallel cites, subsequent history, dates, inferred court, and component spans. Fields: type, volume, reporter, page?, pincite?, pinciteInfo?, court?, unpublished?, normalizedCourt?, year?, normalizedReporter?, groupId?, parallelCitations?, parentheticals?, subsequentHistoryEntries?, subsequentHistoryOf?, date?, possibleInterpretations?, fullSpan?, caseName?, plaintiff?, defendant?, plaintiffNormalized?, defendantNormalized?, proceduralPrefix?, nominativeVolume?, nominativeReporter?, hasBlankPage?, disposition?, justices?, scope?, adminParenthetical?, inferredCourt?, spans?.
- `docket` — `DocketCitation`: docket-number-only case cite (no reporter). Only emitted when a preceding case-name anchor is present. Fields: type, docketNumber, court?, normalizedCourt?, year?, date?, caseName?, plaintiff?, defendant?, plaintiffNormalized?, defendantNormalized?, proceduralPrefix?, fullSpan?.
- `statute` — `StatuteCitation`: U.S.C. and state codes. NOTE: `pincite` here is a string (subsection chain), NOT a page number. Fields: type, title?, code?, section?, sectionRange?, chapter?, subsection?, subsectionRange?, jurisdiction?, pincite?, hasEtSeq?, year?, publisher?, recompiledYear?, editionLabel?, spans?.
- `regulation` — `RegulationCitation`: C.F.R. and state regulatory codes; same shape as StatuteCitation, separate discriminator so consumers filter statutes vs regs without string-matching `code`. Fields: type, title?, code?, section?, sectionRange?, chapter?, subsection?, subsectionRange?, jurisdiction?, pincite?, hasEtSeq?, year?, publisher?, recompiledYear?, editionLabel?, spans?.
- `stateRule` — `StateRuleCitation`: state rules of procedure (ID, NC, SC, plus `CFC` Court of Federal Claims). Fields: type, jurisdiction, ruleSet, rule, subsection?, spans?.
- `journal` — `JournalCitation`: law-review / periodical cites; `pincite` is a number. Fields: type, author?, title?, volume?, journal, abbreviation, page?, pincite?, year?, spans?.
- `neutral` — `NeutralCitation`: vendor-neutral cites. Vendor DBs (`WL`/`LEXIS`/`BL`) live in `database`, NOT in `court`. Fields: type, year, court?, database?, documentNumber, unpublished?, pincite?, pinciteInfo?, date?, caseName?, spans?.
- `publicLaw` — `PublicLawCitation`: federal public laws (`Pub. L. No. 116-283`). Fields: type, congress, lawNumber, title?, spans?.
- `federalRegister` — `FederalRegisterCitation`: `85 Fed. Reg. 12345`. Fields: type, volume, page, year?, spans?.
- `statutesAtLarge` — `StatutesAtLargeCitation`: federal session-law compilation (`100 Stat. 3743`). Fields: type, volume, page, pincite?, pinciteEndPage?, pinciteIsRange?, year?, spans?.
- `sessionLaw` — `SessionLawCitation`: state session laws (CA `Stats.`, NV `Nev. Stat.`). Fields: type, jurisdiction, code, year, chapter, section?, sections?, sectionRange?, page?, pageRange?.
- `treaty` — `TreatyCitation`: treaty-series cites (`T.I.A.S. No. 1502`, `1155 U.N.T.S. 331`); named-treaty fields reserved. Fields: type, series?, seriesNumber?, volume?, page?, treatyName?, article?, paragraph?, year?.
- `legislativeMaterial` — `LegislativeMaterialCitation`: committee reports + Congressional Record, unified via the `kind` discriminator. Fields: type, kind, chamber?, reportNumber?, congress?, session?, volume?, page?, year?.
- `localOrdinance` — `LocalOrdinanceCitation`: municipal codes (`CCCO § 2.12.010(1)`). Fields: type, code, locality?, section.
- `canon` — `CanonCitation`: judicial-conduct canons (`Canon 7(B)(1)`). Fields: type, canon, subsection?, ruleSet?.
- `constitutional` — `ConstitutionalCitation`: U.S. + all 50 states; Roman numerals parsed to integers; `currentLocation` for `former … (now …)` reform cites. Fields: type, jurisdiction?, article?, amendment?, preamble?, section?, clause?, currentLocation?, spans?.
- `federalRule` — `FederalRuleCitation`: federal rules of procedure (civil/criminal/evidence/appellate/bankruptcy). Fields: type, ruleSet, rule, subsection?, spans?.
- `restatement` — `RestatementCitation`: ALI Restatements (`Restatement (Second) of Torts § 402A`). Fields: type, edition, subject, section, subsection?, spans?.
- `treatise` — `TreatiseCitation`: multi-volume treatises (Wright & Miller, Nimmer, etc.). Fields: type, volume, title, section, edition?, year?, spans?.
- `annotation` — `AnnotationCitation`: A.L.R. series — secondary authority that looks like a case cite. Fields: type, series, volume, page, year?, spans?.
- `id` — `IdCitation`: `Id.` / `Id. at N`; `pincite` is a number; carries pincite-inheritance + (when resolved) inherited case-name fields. Fields: type, pincite?, pinciteInfo?, pinciteInherited?, pinciteInheritedFrom?, parenthetical?, caseName?, plaintiff?, defendant?, plaintiffNormalized?, defendantNormalized?, proceduralPrefix?, spans?.
- `supra` — `SupraCitation`: `Party, supra`; resolves by party-name match. Fields: type, partyName?, pincite?, pinciteInfo?, pinciteInherited?, pinciteInheritedFrom?, parenthetical?, spans?.
- `shortFormCase` — `ShortFormCaseCitation`: `500 F.2d at 125`; resolves by volume/reporter match; may carry an inferred case name. Fields: type, volume, reporter, page?, pincite?, pinciteInfo?, pinciteInherited?, pinciteInheritedFrom?, inferredCaseName?, inferredPlaintiff?, inferredDefendant?, inferredCaseNameSpan?, spans?, partyName?, partyNameNormalized?, parenthetical?.

### Common fields (`CitationBase`, on every citation)

text, span, confidence, matchedText, processTimeMs, patternsChecked, warnings?, signal?, stringCitationGroupId?, stringCitationIndex?, stringCitationGroupSize?, inFootnote?, footnoteNumber?.

`inFootnote`/`footnoteNumber` are only populated when `detectFootnotes: true`. On every citation that carries a volume, `volume` is `number | string` — string for hyphenated volumes like `"1984-1"`. Don't assume a number.

### `pinciteInfo` — structured pincite

`pincite: number` on a citation is a convenience mirror of the primary page only. The full structure lives on `pinciteInfo?: PinciteInfo` (case, neutral, id, supra, shortFormCase):

```typescript
interface PinciteInfo {
  page?: number  // Primary page number.
  endPage?: number  // End page for ranges: "570-75" → 575
  footnote?: number  // Footnote number: "570 n.3" → 3.
  footnoteEnd?: number  // End footnote for multi-note refs: "570 nn.3-5" → 5
  isRange: boolean  // True if this is a page or paragraph range
  starPage?: boolean  // True when the pincite uses star-pagination (e.g., "*2"), denoting a slip-opinion page or unreported-decision page rather than a reporter page.
  paragraph?: number  // Paragraph number for `¶ N` / `para. N` pincites (#204).
  endParagraph?: number  // End paragraph for `¶¶ N-M` / `paras. N-M` pincites (#204).
  additionalPincites?: PinciteInfo[]  // Additional discrete pincites following the primary one (#247).
  raw: string  // Original text before parsing
}
```

`parsePincite(raw: string): PinciteInfo | null` is exported for parsing pincite strings directly.

## Core API (`eyecite-ts`)

### Extraction

```typescript
import { extractCitations, extractCitationsAsync } from "eyecite-ts"

const citations = extractCitations(text, options?)               // Citation[]
const resolved  = extractCitations(text, { resolve: true })      // ResolvedCitation[] (overload)
const citations = await extractCitationsAsync(text, options?)    // Promise<Citation[]>
```

`extractCitations` is synchronous. `extractCitationsAsync` currently wraps the sync path (it does NOT auto-load the reporter DB — call `await loadReporters()` explicitly for DB-backed validation). Both have a `{ resolve: true }` overload that narrows the return to `ResolvedCitation[]`.

### `ExtractOptions`

```typescript
interface ExtractOptions {
  cleaners?: Array<(text: string) => string>  // Custom text cleaners (overrides defaults).
  patterns?: Pattern[]  // Custom regex patterns (overrides defaults).
  resolve?: boolean  // Resolve short-form citations to their full antecedents (default: false).
  resolutionOptions?: ResolutionOptions  // Options for citation resolution (only used if resolve: true).
  filterFalsePositives?: boolean  // Remove citations flagged as likely false positives (default: false).
  detectFootnotes?: boolean  // Detect footnote zones and annotate citations with inFootnote/footnoteNumber (default: false)
}
```

> The JSDoc on `ExtractOptions.cleaners` describes a shorter default chain; the **actual** runtime default is the 11-cleaner chain documented under "Cleaners" below (it comes from `cleanText`'s default parameter, which `extractCitations` falls through to).

### False-positive filtering

```typescript
import { applyFalsePositiveFilters } from "eyecite-ts"

// remove=false → penalize confidence (0.1) + add a warning; remove=true → drop flagged cites.
// Pass originalText whenever possible — it is required for the line-crossing FP check; omitting it
// silently skips that check (and warns once per process if case cites are present).
const filtered = applyFalsePositiveFilters(citations, /* remove */ true, originalText)
```

Detection uses a static blocklist of non-US reporter abbreviations plus a year-plausibility heuristic (pre-1750). The `filterFalsePositives: true` extract option runs this in remove mode.

### Resolution

```typescript
import { resolveCitations, DocumentResolver } from "eyecite-ts"

const resolved = resolveCitations(citations, text, options?)   // ResolvedCitation[]
// or, equivalently, the class:
const resolved = new DocumentResolver(citations, text, options?).resolve()
// or inline via extractCitations(text, { resolve: true })
```

`ResolutionOptions`:

```typescript
interface ResolutionOptions {
  scopeStrategy?: ScopeStrategy  // Scope boundary strategy (default: 'none') - none: Resolve across entire document (recommended for HTML-stripped text) - paragraph: Only resolve within same paragraph (too…
  autoDetectParagraphs?: boolean  // Auto-detect paragraph boundaries from text (default: true) Uses paragraphBoundaryPattern to split text
  paragraphBoundaryPattern?: RegExp  // Regex pattern to detect paragraph boundaries (default: /\n\n+/) Only used if autoDetectParagraphs is true
  fuzzyPartyMatching?: boolean  // Enable fuzzy party name matching for supra resolution (default: true) Uses Levenshtein distance to handle typos and variations
  partyMatchThreshold?: number  // Similarity threshold for fuzzy party matching (default: 0.8) Range: 0-1 where 1.0 is exact match Only used if fuzzyPartyMatching is true
  idConfidenceFloor?: number  // Optional confidence floor for `Id.` resolution (default: unset — always commit).
  reportUnresolved?: boolean  // Report unresolved citations with failure reasons (default: true) If false: resolution field will be undefined for unresolved citations
  footnoteMap?: FootnoteMap | undefined  // Footnote zone map for footnote-aware scoping.
}
```

`ResolutionResult` (on `resolvedCitation.resolution`):

```typescript
interface ResolutionResult {
  resolvedTo?: number  // Index of the citation this resolves to. undefined if resolution failed
  antecedentIndex?: number  // Index of this short-form's antecedent. - SUCCESS path (`resolvedTo` defined): mirrors `resolvedTo`, so consumers have one source of truth for what the short-form points at — even…
  failureReason?: string  // Reason for resolution failure (if any)
  warnings?: string[]  // Warnings about ambiguous or uncertain resolutions
  confidence: number  // Confidence in the resolution (0-1) Factors: party name similarity, scope boundary, citation type match
}
```

`resolvedTo` (and `antecedentIndex`) are **indices, not citation objects** — look up via `citations[c.resolution.resolvedTo]`. `ResolvedCitation<C>` is a distributive conditional type: `resolution` is meaningfully present only on short-form citations; on full citations it is typed `undefined`.

Resolver behavior worth knowing (0.29.x): `Id.` and `supra` skip parenthetical-internal asides (`(quoting X)` / `(citing Y)`) as antecedents; the aside signal is trigger-anchored and bracket-balance-tolerant, so a dropped/garbled paren (OCR/PDF) no longer mis-scopes a whole document. On a bracket-balance failure the paren-child exclusion degrades to *soft* (candidate kept, confidence capped, warning emitted) so `idConfidenceFloor` can abstain. `supra` abstains on a true non-unique party-name key (same name + year) and degrades-with-warning otherwise. Pincites inherit from the immediately-preceding same-authority citation per Bluebook 4.1 (`pinciteInherited`/`pinciteInheritedFrom`).

### Document understanding — `analyzeDocument`

A pure projection over an already-extracted citation array (no re-tokenization; sub-millisecond for typical briefs). This is how you get **quote attribution** and the **relationships between citations**.

```typescript
import { extractCitations, analyzeDocument } from "eyecite-ts"
import type { Document, QuoteAttribution, CitationGraph, Edge } from "eyecite-ts"

const citations = extractCitations(text)
const doc: Document = analyzeDocument(text, citations, { transformationMap? })
```

```typescript
interface Document {
  citations: Citation[]  // The citations that were analyzed.
  proseSpans: Span[]  // Prose between citations (+ before-first + after-last).
  precedingProse: Map<number, Span>  // Per-citation view: prose span ending at this citation.
  followingProse: Map<number, Span>  // Per-citation view: prose span starting after this citation.
  quoteAttributions: QuoteAttribution[]  // Detected quoted-text zones with attempted attribution.
  citationGraph: CitationGraph  // All relationships between citations as typed edges.
  footnoteZones?: AnalyzedFootnoteZone[]  // Footnote zones with citation members.
}
```

#### Quote attribution

```typescript
type AttributionKind = "block-quote" | "adjacent" | "parenthetical"

interface QuoteAttribution {
  quoteSpan: Span  // The quoted-text span in original-text coordinates.
  quoteText: string  // Verbatim quoted text (chars between the marks, exclusive of the marks).
  citationIndex?: number  // Citation index that vouches for the quote; undefined when none found.
  attributionKind?: AttributionKind  // How the attribution was inferred; undefined iff citationIndex is.
  confidence?: number  // Confidence (0-1).
}
```

#### Citation graph — 7 edge types

```typescript
interface CitationGraph {
  nodes: number[]
  edges: Edge[]
}
```

`from`/`to` are indices into `Document.citations`. `Edge` is a discriminated union of **7 kinds**:

1. `resolves-to` — `{ type, from, to, confidence, warnings? }` — short-form → resolved antecedent (from `resolution.resolvedTo`)
2. `antecedent` — `{ type, from, to }` — short-form → its antecedent (from `resolution.antecedentIndex`)
3. `parallel` — `{ type, from, to, groupId }` — same case across reporters (one edge per pair in a group)
4. `history-of` — `{ type, from, to, signal }` — subsequent-history link (from `subsequentHistoryOf`)
5. `pincite-inherit` — `{ type, from, to }` — pincite inherited from `to` (from `pinciteInheritedFrom`)
6. `string-cite` — `{ type, from, to, groupId, position }` — adjacent members of a semicolon string-cite group
7. `in-parenthetical-of` — `{ type, from, to }` — `from` is cited inside `to`'s explanatory parenthetical (e.g. `(quoting X)`); balance-tolerant

`AnalyzedFootnoteZone`: `{ start, end, footnoteNumber, citationIndices }`.

### Footnote detection

```typescript
import { detectFootnotes } from "eyecite-ts"
import type { FootnoteMap, FootnoteZone } from "eyecite-ts"

const map: FootnoteMap = detectFootnotes(text)   // auto-detects HTML or plain-text footnotes
// or integrate: extractCitations(text, { detectFootnotes: true }) → cites tagged inFootnote/footnoteNumber
```

### Type guards

```typescript
import { assertUnreachable, isCaseCitation, isCitationType, isFullCitation, isShortFormCitation } from "eyecite-ts"

isFullCitation(citation)              // narrows to FullCitation
isShortFormCitation(citation)         // narrows to ShortFormCitation (id|supra|shortFormCase)
isCaseCitation(citation)              // narrows to FullCaseCitation
isCitationType(citation, "statute")   // generic guard, narrows to CitationOfType<"statute">
assertUnreachable(x)                  // exhaustiveness check for switch defaults
```

### Granular / power-user API

```typescript
import {
  cleanText, tokenize, parsePincite, normalizeCourt, spanFromGroupIndex,
  extractAnnotation, extractCase, extractConstitutional, extractFederalRegister, extractFederalRule, extractJournal, extractNeutral, extractPublicLaw, extractRestatement, extractStatute, extractStatutesAtLarge, extractTreatise,
} from "eyecite-ts"

const { cleaned, transformationMap, warnings } = cleanText(text, cleaners?)  // CleanTextResult
const tokens = tokenize(cleaned, patterns?)                                   // Token[]
```

Exactly **12 individual extractors** are exported (the list above). Note: `extractStatute` returns `StatuteCitation | RegulationCitation`. Types like `docket`, `stateRule`, `sessionLaw`, `treaty`, `legislativeMaterial`, `localOrdinance`, and `canon` are produced internally by `extractCitations` but have **no standalone extractor** in the public surface.

## Cleaners

The cleaning layer is composable, but its public surface is intentionally narrow.

- **Exported:** `cleanText(original, cleaners?)` — the orchestrator — and the `CleanTextResult` type (`{ cleaned, transformationMap, warnings }`). That's all.
- **NOT exported:** the individual cleaner functions (`stripHtmlTags`, `decodeHtmlEntities`, `rejoinHyphenatedWords`, `stripPageBreakMarkers`, `replaceWhitespace`, `collapseSpaces`, `normalizeUnicode`, `normalizeDashes`, `fixSmartQuotes`, `normalizeTypography`, `normalizeReporterSpacing`, plus the opt-in `removeOcrArtifacts`, `stripDiacritics`, and legacy `normalizeWhitespace`). They live in `src/clean/cleaners.ts`; no public package entry re-exports them and there is no `eyecite-ts/clean` entry. To supply a custom `cleaners` array, write the functions yourself (each is just `(text: string) => string`).

**Default cleaner chain** (applied by `cleanText`, and therefore by `extractCitations` when no `cleaners` option is given), in order — derived from `cleanText`'s default parameter:

```
stripHtmlTags → decodeHtmlEntities → rejoinHyphenatedWords → stripPageBreakMarkers → replaceWhitespace → collapseSpaces → normalizeUnicode → normalizeDashes → fixSmartQuotes → normalizeTypography → normalizeReporterSpacing
```

**Gotcha — `cleaners` REPLACES, it does not augment.** Passing `options.cleaners` (or a `cleaners` argument to `cleanText`) substitutes the entire default chain. If you pass `{ cleaners: [myCleaner] }`, HTML stripping, Unicode/dash normalization, smart-quote fixing, etc. **no longer run**. To keep the defaults plus your own, you must reconstruct the full chain (and, since the built-ins aren't exported, reimplement the ones you need).

## Annotation (`eyecite-ts/annotate`)

```typescript
import { annotate } from "eyecite-ts/annotate"
import type { AnnotationOptions, AnnotationResult } from "eyecite-ts/annotate"

// Template mode
const r1 = annotate(text, citations, { template: { before: '<cite>', after: '</cite>' } })

// Callback mode (full control)
const r2 = annotate(text, citations, {
  callback: (citation, surrounding) => `<a href="...">${citation.matchedText}</a>`,
})
```

`AnnotationOptions`:

```typescript
interface AnnotationOptions {
  useCleanText?: boolean  // Apply annotations to cleaned text (true) or original text (false). - `true`: Use citation.span.cleanStart/End positions - `false`: Use citation.span.originalStart/End positions
  autoEscape?: boolean  // Auto-escape HTML entities to prevent XSS injection.
  useFullSpan?: boolean  // Use full citation span from case name through parenthetical (true) or core citation only (false).
  callback?: (citation: C, surrounding: string) => string  // Callback for custom annotation logic.
  template?: { before: string; after: string }  // Template mode: simple before/after markup strings.
}
```

`AnnotationResult`:

```typescript
interface AnnotationResult {
  text: string  // Annotated text with markup inserted at citation positions.
  positionMap: Map<number, number>  // Position mapping from original positions to annotated positions.
  skipped: Citation[]  // Citations that couldn't be annotated. Populated when: - Two citation wraps overlap (intersect or nest). The outer/higher- confidence wrap wins; the other surfaces here (#543…
}
```

## Utils (`eyecite-ts/utils`)

```typescript
import { getSurroundingContext, groupByCase, toBluebook, toDurableLocator, toDurableLocators, toReporterKey, toReporterKeys } from "eyecite-ts/utils"
import type { CaseGroup, ContextOptions, DurableLocator, DurableLocatorOptions, SurroundingContext } from "eyecite-ts/utils"

toReporterKey(caseCitation)    // "550 U.S. 544" (FullCaseCitation → string; omits page for blank-page cites)
toReporterKeys(caseCitation)   // ["410 U.S. 113", "93 S. Ct. 705"] (primary + parallel keys)
toBluebook(citation)           // canonical Bluebook string; works across all citation types
groupByCase(resolvedCitations) // CaseGroup[]; takes ResolvedCitation[]; ignores non-case + unresolved short-forms
getSurroundingContext(text, { start, end }, { type: "sentence" | "paragraph", maxLength?: 500 })
```

`getSurroundingContext` is legal-text-aware: periods in `U.S.`, `Corp.`, `F.3d`, `v.`, `No.`, etc. are not treated as sentence boundaries.

```typescript
interface CaseGroup {
  primaryCitation: FullCaseCitation  // The first full citation encountered for this case
  mentions: ResolvedCitation[]  // All mentions (full, short, id, supra) in document order
  parallelCitations: string[]  // Distinct reporter strings: ["550 U.S. 544", "127 S. Ct. 1955"]
}
```

## Data (`eyecite-ts/data`)

The reporter DB is a separate, lazy-loaded entry. Core extraction works without it (pattern-based). Loading it adds reporter validation.

```typescript
import { findAbbreviatedCode, findNamedCode, findReportersByAbbreviation, getReportersSync, loadReporters, abbreviatedCodes, namedCodes, stateStatuteEntries } from "eyecite-ts/data"
import type { CodeEntry, ReporterEdition, ReporterEntry, ReportersDatabase, StateStatuteEntry } from "eyecite-ts/data"

const db = await loadReporters()                  // Promise<ReportersDatabase>; cached after first load
const cached = getReportersSync()                 // ReportersDatabase | null (null in degraded mode)
const reporters = await findReportersByAbbreviation("F.2d")  // ReporterEntry[] ([] if none)
findNamedCode("CA", "Civ. Proc.")                 // CodeEntry | undefined (longest-match wins)
findAbbreviatedCode("MCL")                         // CodeEntry | undefined
```

`ReportersDatabase`: `{ byAbbreviation, all }` (1200+ reporters; case-insensitive lookup keys).

## Supported jurisdictions (statutes / regs / rules)

- Federal: U.S.C., C.F.R., prose ("section X of title Y")
- Named-code states: NY, CA, TX, MD, VA, AL, MA
- Abbreviated-code states: FL, OH, MI, UT, CO, WA, NC, GA, PA, IN, NJ, DE, and more (see `stateStatuteEntries`)
- Chapter-act: IL (ILCS); Massachusetts chapter+section (`G.L. c. 93A`)
- Constitutional: U.S. + all 50 states; Roman numerals parsed to integers; `former … (now …)` reform cites
- State rules of procedure: ID, NC, SC, plus the Court of Federal Claims (`CFC`)

## Development (in the eyecite-ts repo)

```bash
pnpm install            # corepack + pnpm 10
pnpm exec vitest run    # run tests once (pnpm test = watch)
pnpm build              # tsdown (ESM + CJS + DTS); prebuild regenerates reporters data + this file
pnpm typecheck          # tsc --noEmit
pnpm lint               # Biome
pnpm size               # size-limit
pnpm llms:generate      # regenerate llms.txt from the API surface (this file)
```
